MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Aug-03-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHARON SCHMEISER's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND:

Defendant Sharon T. Schmeiser's motion to set aside default judgment and quash service for lack of jurisdiction is denied.

Defendant is a dentist in Miami, Florida. Plaintiff argues that the forum selection clause contained in its Align Technology Pricing Terms and Conditions ("Terms") for USA provides a basis for personal jurisdiction, agreed to with every order. (Henry Decl., Exs. B-G ["Customer and Align agree that the courts of the State of California shall have exclusive jurisdiction over any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with these Terms and contract, or their or its subject matter or formation (Dispute) . . . . "].) The Terms define the "Customer" as "the doctor whose Invisalign customer identity number (allocated to a single individual only) is used to access the password protected Align Systems ('Invisalign Doctor')." They also state, "If a practice, partnership, corporate body or other legal entity for or with which the Invisalign Doctor works is named on Align Invoice(s) and/or that practice pays Align's invoices, then Align is entitled to assume that it is the Invisalign Doctor who uses Align's Systems and places Customer Orders with the authorization of, for and on behalf of that entity (the 'Practice'). Under these circumstances, the Practice will also be Align's Customer and the Invisalign Doctor and the Practice will be jointly and severally liable for obligations in these Terms which are obligations of the 'Customer'."

"[I]t is settled . . . that parties to a contract may agree in advance to submit to the jurisdiction of a given court." (Nat'l Equip. Rental, Ltd. v. Szukhent (1964) 375 U.S. 311, 315-316; see also Rockefeller Technology Investments (Asia) VII v. Changzhou Sinotype Technology Co., Ltd. (2020) 9 Cal.5th 125, 140 ["Agreeing to resolve a particular dispute in a specific jurisdiction, for example, is one means of expressing consent to personal jurisdiction of courts in the forum state for purposes of that dispute. While subject matter jurisdiction cannot be conferred by consent, personal jurisdiction can be so conferred, and consent may be given by a contract provision." (citations and internal quotations omitted)].) Plaintiff's evidence shows that Defendant ordered aligners or other materials from it using her name and password, and that all of the orders were billed and shipped to Kendall Breeze Dental Center, a dba used by defendant's professional limited liability corporation. (Henry Decl. 5-8, 12, 14-19 & Ex. A [jnvoices]; Cook Decl., Ex. B.) Defendant is the sole member and managing member of that PLLC. (Cook Decl., Ex. B at 25, 28.) By placing orders with Plaintiff, Defendant as Customer agreed to the Terms, and thereby voluntarily consented to jurisdiction in California on her own behalf and on behalf of her PLLC, as well as agreeing that her PLLC would be jointly and severally liable for those obligations.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Sharon T. Schmeiser, an individual, dba Kendall Breeze Dental Center.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

David J. Cook, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Align Technology Inc., a Delaware corporation.
Kent Alleyne, Senior Credit & Collections Manager at Align Technology Inc., was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. Cook and Alleyne appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Sharon T. Schmeiser, an individual, dba Kendall Breeze Dental Center.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Open Book Account, Account Stated and Quantum Meriut was filed on October 2, 2020, and personally served on defendant Sharon T. Schmeiser, an individual, dba Kendall Breeze Dental Center, on October 14, 2020.
Default as to defendant Sharon T. Schmeiser, an individual, dba Kendall Breeze Dental Center, was entered on November 17, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on December 4, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Align Technology Inc., a Delaware corporation, and against the defaulted defendant Sharon T. Schmeiser, an individual, dba Kendall Breeze Dental Center, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $42,271.45.
Prejudgment Interest - $1,980.18.
Attorney Fees - $1,000.00.
Costs - $640.00.

Total - $45,891.63.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
